steps.title: Set up
steps.content: Both pyramids have square bases with area 100 cm², so s = 10 cm. The slant height l of each pyramid uses the Pythagorean theorem with the pyramid height h and half the base (5 cm): $$l = \sqrt{h^2 + 5^2}$$ Pyramid 1 (h = 11): $l_1 = \sqrt{121 + 25} = \sqrt{146}$   Pyramid 2 (h = 22): $l_2 = \sqrt{484 + 25} = \sqrt{509}$

steps.title: Solve
steps.content: Each pyramid has 4 triangular faces, each with area (1/2)(10)(l) = 5l. Total lateral area difference: $$20\sqrt{509} - 20\sqrt{146} \approx 20(22.56 - 12.08) \approx 209.6 \text{ cm}^2$$
